today_news.title: Anthropic's new prompt forces ChatGPT to reveal everything it knows about you
today_news.summary: Anthropic is capitalizing on OpenAI's bad press with a new import function for Claude. A single prompt exports your saved context from ChatGPT or other chatbots, letting you transfer it straight to Claude's memory.

today_news.title: OpenAI’s “compromise” with the Pentagon is what Anthropic feared
today_news.summary: On February 28, OpenAI announced it had reached a deal that will allow the US military to use its technologies in classified settings. CEO Sam Altman said the negotiations, which the company began pursuing only after the Pentagon’s public reprimand of Anthropic, were “definitely rushed.” In its anno...

today_news.title: ASML plans to expand beyond chip lithography into advanced packaging
today_news.summary: ASML, the world's sole manufacturer of EUV lithography machines used to produce advanced chips, is looking to expand beyond its core business.

today_news.title: The Download: protesting AI, and what’s floating in space

today_news.title: I checked out one of the biggest anti-AI protests yet
today_news.summary: Pull the plug! Pull the plug! Stop the slop! Stop the slop! For a few hours this Saturday, February 28, I watched as a couple of hundred anti-AI protesters marched through London’s King’s Cross tech hub, home to the UK headquarters of OpenAI, Meta, and Google DeepMind, chanting slogans and waving si...

today_news.title: Thousands of procurement documents show how China's army wants to weaponize AI
today_news.summary: Researchers at Georgetown University have analyzed thousands of procurement requests from China's People's Liberation Army. The documents reveal how broadly Beijing is already experimenting with military AI, from drone swarms and deepfake tools to autonomous decision-making systems.

today_news.title: Inside the Anthropic-Pentagon breakdown: mass surveillance, autonomous weapons, and a rival deal waiting in the wings
today_news.summary: New reports from the New York Times and the Atlantic paint a detailed picture of the final hours of negotiations between Anthropic and the Pentagon. At the center: bulk data collection on American citizens, a rejected cloud workaround, and a parallel OpenAI deal that was already in the works.

today_tech.title: VoxKnesset: A Large-Scale Longitudinal Hebrew Speech Dataset for Aging Speaker Modeling
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Speech processing systems face a fundamental challenge: the human voice changes with age, yet few datasets support rigorous longitudinal evaluation. today_tech.title: Initialization-Aware Score-Based Diffusion Sampling
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Score-based generative models (SGMs) aim at generating samples from a target distribution by approximating the reverse-time dynamics of a stochastic differential equation. today_tech.title: EmCoop: A Framework and Benchmark for Embodied Cooperation Among LLM Agents
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Real-world scenarios increasingly require multiple embodied agents to collaborate in dynamic environments under embodied constraints, as many tasks exceed the capabilities of any single agent. today_tech.title: CARE: Confounder-Aware Aggregation for Reliable LLM Evaluation
today_tech.summary: Abstract: LLM-as-a-judge ensembles are the standard paradigm for scalable evaluation, but their aggregation mechanisms suffer from a fundamental flaw: they implicitly assume that judges provide independent estimates of true quality. today_tech.title: Attn-QAT: 4-Bit Attention With Quantization-Aware Training
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Achieving reliable 4-bit attention is a prerequisite for end-to-end FP4 computation on emerging FP4-capable GPUs, yet attention remains the main obstacle due to FP4's tiny dynamic range and attention's heavy-tailed activations.
